name: Frozen Peach Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and slice about 1 to 1 1/2 lbs. (enough to make 1 cup) of the peaches. Place sliced peaches in a food processor, food mill, or blender to puree the peaches. Refrigerate the puree.
2. In the top of a double boiler, beat egg yolks with 3/4 cup sugar until thick and lemony in color. Stir in the lemon juice and peach liqueur. Cook over hot water, stirring constantly, until the mixture has thickened, about 4 minutes. Cool to room temperature and fold in the peach puree. Chill in the refrigerator.
3. In a mixing bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, beating until meringue is stiff. Fold into the peach mixture. Spoon the peach mousse into 8 custard cups and freeze.
4. Before serving, peel and slice the remaining peaches. To serve, unmold the frozen mousse onto individual dessert plates and garnish with sliced peaches. The mousse tastes best if allowed to stand and soften for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
